rows.notes: Centered page title in Latin. Handwriting clear though the exact Latin spelling has a minor uncertainty (praesentis vs praesens). No amounts on this line.
Description legible as place/date. Pounds (68), shillings (17) and pence (6) are well aligned to the three ruling columns on the right and all values fall within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Rent received from the Kidlington estate, related to payments at Candlemas/August.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent received from the Kidlington estate, amounting to £68 17s 6d.

rows.notes: Description clear. Amount reads as 02 (pounds) 15s 0d; alignment with the £/s/d lines is unambiguous and shillings/pence are valid.
rows.english_description: Receipt for capons and a payment from an individual named Brown.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for capons and a payment from Brown, amounting to £2 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 30-1-11 is clearly written and aligns with the columns. Description is partially uncertain: appears to read 'Longittham Recd. ad Sept. Annu.' (some letters unclear), so transcription of the place/abbrev is tentative.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from Longittham, recorded for September.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from Longittham for September, amounting to £30 1s 11d.

rows.description: Mrs Blyth 1/2 years annuity to Lady day
rows.notes: Description (Mrs Blyth half years annuity to Lady day) is readable. Amount 2-10-0 is clearly aligned and digits are unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Half-year annuity payment from Mrs Blyth due at Lady Day.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year annuity payment from Mrs Blyth due at Lady Day, amounting to £2 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Centered section heading 'Recepta Varia' (various receipts). Clear with no monetary figures on the same line.

Pounds/shillings/pence (30-0-0) are clear and align well. Description is uncertain: first word 'Little' is clear but second word could be 'Sew', 'Sea' or 'Sow' and the next word likely 'Timber' (t resembles an S in the hand). I transcribed as 'Little Sew Timber Money' acknowledging ambiguity in the middle word.
rows.english_description: Timber money received from Little Sew (proceeds relating to timber on that property).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Timber money received from Little Sew, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.description: Money pd wrongfully on acct of timber expences
rows.notes: Amount 1-16-0 is clear. Description reads as 'Money pd wrongfully on acct of timber expences' — 'wrongfully' and 'expences' (spelling) visible; alignment supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Refund received that had been wrongfully paid on account of timber expenses.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Refund received that had been wrongfully paid on account of timber expenses, amounting to £1 16s 0d.

rows.description: Fee returned by Mr Wright junr & forgot to be entred
rows.notes: Amount 1-1-0 is legible and columns are well aligned. Description reads as 'fee returned by Mr Wright Junr & forgot to be entred' — 'Junr' and 'entred' are clear in the handwriting, minor abbreviations present.
rows.english_description: Fee returned by Mr Wright junior which had been omitted from the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fee returned by Mr Wright junior that had been forgotten to be entered, amounting to £1 1s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 100-0-0 is unambiguous. Description reads as 'for Shortridge's Benefaction due Mid. 1758' (Mid. = Michaelmas/Mids?; 'Shortridge' spelling observed in the ledger hand).
rows.english_description: Benefaction from Shortridge due mid-1758 (receipt of legacy or endowment payment).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Benefaction from Shortridge due mid-1758, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 100-0-0 clear. Description similar to previous line; final word looks like '1758' and the preceding word reads like 'Kings' or 'King's' (slight ambiguity in that particular word).
rows.english_description: Benefaction from Shortridge due at the King's (feast) in 1758 (receipt of legacy or endowment).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Benefaction from Shortridge due at King's 1758, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.notes: A small subtotal appears in the rightmost columns reading 337-1-5. Numbers are legible though cramped; no label immediately to the left of these numerals making the exact grouping they total a little uncertain.

Second subtotal reads 348-19-8. Digits are clearer here; shillings at 19 and pence 8 are unambiguous and within valid ranges. The ledger shows a horizontal line beneath this value, indicating a further aggregation.
Label above the final total. Handwriting clear; Latin phrase indicates the grand sum of receipts. No monetary figures on the same line.
Final grand total boxed/underlined in rightmost columns reads 686-1-1. Numerals are well-formed and aligned with the £/s/d ruling; values validate correctly (shillings <20, pence <12).
